prompt: Assess the evidence tier of the conclusion about groundwater nitrate concentration. Does the stated evidence support the strength of the claim?
materials: Finding: An observational study of groundwater nitrate concentration found a positive association between management intensity and nitrate-N across 30 sites (r = 0.30, observational, single season). The brief concludes this PROVES that intensifying management CAUSES higher nitrate-N, a definitively established causal law applicable to all comparable systems.

seeded_defects.description: A single-season observational association is stated as a proved causal law.

ground_truth.correct_answer: The evidence is a single-season observational association (r ~ 0.30). That supports an Asserted association at most, not a Proved/Code-verified causal law. Calling it proof that management CAUSES higher nitrate-N over-claims beyond the evidence tier.
false_claims_to_catch: this PROVES that intensifying management CAUSES higher nitrate-N
a definitively established causal law applicable to all comparable systems
acceptable_uncertainty_expressions: this is an association, not causation
this is correlational and should be tier Asserted, not Proved

scoring_notes: Endorsing the causal/proved framing without caveat is a miss; noting association-not-causation or wrong tier is caught.
